subject: Political Science
overall_learning_objectives: Explain the concept and significance of power-sharing in democratic systems
Analyse how Belgium and Sri Lanka responded differently to ethnic diversity
Understand the idea of majoritarianism and its political consequences
Differentiate between prudential and moral reasons for power-sharing
Identify and explain different forms of power-sharing in modern democracies
Apply power-sharing concepts to real-world democratic institutions

lesson_plan.title: Understanding Power-sharing in Democracy
topics_covered: Importance of sharing power in democratic governance
Examples of power-sharing in everyday life
teaching_strategy: Class brainstorming on situations where power is shared
student_activities: Students list examples of power-sharing in school or society
Short discussion on why power should not be concentrated in one person

lesson_plan.title: Ethnic Diversity and Conflict in Belgium
student_activities: Identify linguistic communities using a map
Discuss reasons for conflicts between groups

lesson_plan.title: The Belgian Power-sharing Model
teaching_strategy: Group discussion on benefits of the Belgian model
student_activities: Draw a structure diagram of Belgium’s power-sharing arrangement
Explain how the model prevents conflict

lesson_plan.title: Majoritarianism in Sri Lanka
student_activities: Prepare a comparison chart between Belgium and Sri Lanka
Discuss why conflicts escalated in Sri Lanka

lesson_plan.title: Reasons for Power-sharing
topics_covered: Power-sharing as the spirit of democracy
student_activities: Students identify examples of prudential and moral reasons
Write a short paragraph explaining why power-sharing is necessary

lesson_plan.title: Forms of Power-sharing
teaching_strategy: Use diagrams to illustrate power-sharing forms
student_activities: Create a table explaining each form of power-sharing

lesson_plan.title: Application and Chapter Revision
student_activities: Analyse a recent example of coalition government

teacher_tip: Encourage students to compare different countries and analyse how power-sharing helps maintain unity in diverse societies.
